senses.definition: to assign something to a different person, group, or purpose
examples: The company reassigns staff to new projects as needed.
They reassigned the manager to oversee the European division.

senses.definition: to rejoin and adapt oneself to a society, culture, or group after being separated or away
examples: refugees attempt to reassimilate into their communities after years abroad.
The returnee struggled to reassimilate into everyday life.

senses.definition: having rejoined and adapted oneself to a society, culture, or group after being separated or away
examples: Many returnees felt reassimilated after a few months back home.
The community reassimilated the newcomers with support programs.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reassimilate; to rejoin and adapt oneself to a society, culture, or group after being separated or away
examples: The veteran reassimilates into civilian life with the help of a program.
Exiles reassimilates into their homeland culture over time.

senses.definition: present participle of reassimilate; actively rejoining and adapting to a group or society after separation
examples: They are reassimilating into the local culture after years abroad.
Programs exist to help families reassimilating into the community.

senses.definition: the act or process of reassimilating; the state of becoming integrated again into a group or society
examples: The reassimulation of the refugee community took several years.
Support services aid the reassimulation process.

senses.definition: to assist again or provide additional help to someone
examples: The nurse reassists the patient with walking drills.
They reassist the team by supplying extra equipment.

senses.definition: the act of assisting again or the provision of extra help
examples: The project received reassistance from international donors.
Reassistance was offered to all affected workers.

senses.definition: to associate again or form new associations after a change or interruption
examples: Researchers reassociate the data with updated hypotheses.
They reassociate the brands with new campaigns.

senses.definition: having associated again or formed new associations after a change
examples: The brands were reassociated with fresh imagery.
The memories were reassociated with the new context.

senses.definition: present participle of reassociate; forming new or renewed associations
examples: The team is reassociating with the new branding strategy.
They are reassociating memories with the new era.

senses.definition: the act of associating again or the state of forming renewed associations
examples: There was a reassociation of teams after the merger.
Reassociation of the memories occurred with new context.

senses.definition: to reallocate or re-mix items, especially in contexts like playing cards or products
examples: They reassort the deck before dealing.
The factory reassorts products to balance inventory.

senses.definition: having been reallocated or re-mixed
examples: The cards were reassorted into a new order.
Reassorted products improved stock flow.

senses.definition: present participle of reassort; rearranging or re-mixing items to create a new arrangement
examples: The team is reassorting the components for efficiency.
They are reassorting seats for the meeting.

senses.definition: the act or result of reassorting; a new arrangement or grouping
examples: The sneeze influenza virus often undergoes reassortment.
Reassortment of product lines created a better mix.

senses.definition: plural of reassortment; multiple instances of reallocation or re-mixing
examples: Various reassortments of the schedule occurred during the week.
The virus shows several reassortments in different strains.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reassort; to rearrange or reallocate items
examples: The team reassorts duties to cover all shifts.
They reassorts the product lineup to match demand.

senses.definition: to take up again a role, position, or responsibility after a period away
examples: She reassumes her duties after a brief leave.
The executive reassumes leadership following the investment.

senses.definition: having taken up again a role, position, or responsibility
examples: He reassumed the post after the interim period.
She reassumed control of the project.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reassume; to take up again a role or responsibility
examples: The manager reassumes authority after the review.
She reassumes her responsibilities promptly.

senses.definition: present participle of reassume; taking up again a role or responsibility
examples: He is reassuming his role after the leave.
They are reassuming duties as planned.

senses.definition: the act of resuming a role or responsibility or the act of taking something back again
examples: The reassumption of duties occurred smoothly.
Reassumption of leadership followed the retirement.

senses.definition: plural of reassumption; multiple acts of resuming roles or beliefs
examples: The reassumptions of different responsibilities occurred over several weeks.
Reassumptions of policy commitments were announced.

senses.definition: comfort or confidence given to someone that something is true or will happen
examples: Her words gave me reassurance about my job interview.
They sought reassurance from the doctor.
